name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value exceeding the original input

name: align_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        a <= usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 should be the identity function since every integer is 1-aligned

name: aligned_not_fixpoint
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_not_fixpoint(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned value must be a fixpoint of align_to_usize

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0nat, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every positive number so it must always be aligned

name: align_result_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_result_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize must itself be aligned to b by construction
